decisionSummary: Custom tbd-sat v1 container: 'TBDS' magic + u32 version + u32 jsonLength + JSON index + concatenated lossless-WebP (VP8L) blocks, one block grid per mip level (base tiled 2x2 over 8192px). One fetch -> one immutable rgba8unorm luma.gl texture with the full 14-level mip chain and trilinear sampling on a single BitmapLayer.

full-webp-browser-mips.how: single 12800^2 lossless full.webp as BitmapLayer.image; deck.gl 9.3 createTexture() auto-allocates getMipLevelCount() levels, runs generateMipmapsWebGL(), and samples mipmapFilter:'linear' (verified in @deck.gl/core/dist/utils/texture.js)
why_rejected: no device adaptivity: an 8192-limit GPU must decode the full 655 MB RGBA then browser-resize, exactly on the machines that cannot take the texture
GPU box-filter mips instead of build-time Lanczos
single-threaded decode of one 163 MPx image (the tiled base decodes ~parallel on the browser worker pool)
no self-describing header (worldBounds/provenance/sha) and dead-ends past the VP8L 16383 px dimension cap for larger terrains

why: build-time Lanczos mip chain (magick cascade) stored as lossless VP8L: zero new runtime deps - createImageBitmap decodes WebP natively

device-adaptive base level: pickBaseLevel() starts the texture at the first mip <= maxTextureDimension2D and NEVER decodes skipped levels (8192-limit GPU -> 6400^2 base, ~218 MB VRAM, no 655 MB decode)
base level tiled ceil(dim/8192)^2 -> parallel browser decode of the dominant cost + future >16383 px terrains stay encodable
GLB-style JSON index is self-describing (worldBounds, metersPerPixel, source, inputSha256) and trivially verifiable dep-free (verify-unified-satellite.mjs)

luma.gl 9.3 upload path verified in node_modules: device.createTexture({mipLevels, sampler}) uses immutable texStorage2D; texture.copyExternalImage({image,x,y,width,height,mipLevel}) does per-mip sub-rect upload; deck.gl createTexture() returns an externally-created Texture as-is and never destroys it (we own the lifecycle)

why_rejected: new deps both sides: basis/toktx encoder at build time, @loaders.gl/textures transcoder at runtime
BC7 is lossy - the program locked the picture-perfect lossless satellite (T-090.1.2.1 precedent)
BC7/basis encode of 163 MPx + full mip chain is minutes-to-hours vs 79 s for VP8L

deck.gl 9.3 compressed-texture path forces mipmapFilter:'nearest' when data.length > 1 (utils/texture.js) - kills the smooth trilinear zoom this slice exists for, unless we hand-build the texture anyway (at which point the custom container is simpler)
uncompressed chain is ~873 MB on disk/LFS/wire - VP8L inside the bundle is sanctioned by the spec sketch and 4.2x smaller

measured.input: packages/map-assets/everon/staging/sap/everon-sap-ortho.png (12800^2, 198.6 MB PNG, sha256 embedded in bundle index)

requestsPerPanZoom.pyramid: up to 64 tile layers remounting per LOD change

gpuCost.fullBase: rgba8unorm 12800^2 + chain ~= 873 MB VRAM (desktop gaming audience, maxTextureDimension2D >= 12800 i.e. 16384 typical)
gpuCost.adaptive8192: base level 1 (6400^2) ~= 218 MB VRAM, 655 MB base decode skipped entirely

verification.structuralGate: scripts/map-assets/verify-unified-satellite.mjs TERRAIN=everon -> OK (14 mips, 17 VP8L blocks, dims/offsets/manifest agreement)
verification.decodeProof: dwebp decode of sliced blocks (levels 0,1,6,13) all valid at declared dims
verification.losslessProof: magick compare -metric AE of bundle level-0 quadrant 0 vs source top-left 6400^2 crop = 0 (bit-identical)
verification.serveProof: vite dev HEAD 200, Content-Length 205896703 (determinate progress), payload starts 'TBDS'+v1
